desc:
Towering shelves of books line the walls of this large room. Some of the books
look quite ancient, and some are quite new. A large, finely carved oak table is
in the centre of the room. A plush chair sits on either side of the rectangular
table. One end of the table is covered with many old scrolls, and prophetic
texts, while the other is much more clear, save for some fine works of fiction.
A large spiral stairway leads to the second floor. To the north, a set of wide
double doors open onto a beautiful bridge.
name: A Book Lined Study

desc:
The view from this room may indeed be unsurpassed on arda. Thick crystal walls
surround the chamber, leaving your eyes to wander across the vast ocean to the
west and back to the towers and other buildings of Grey Havens to the east.
Several padded benches are here, providing a comfortable spot to rest regardless
of which view you wish to enjoy. The floor is polished white marble. Several
marble columns carved into great trees seem to grow from the very stone of the
floor.
name: The Crystal Chamber

desc:
This is the middle of a beautiful, wild garden. Flowers of all colours bloom
around, filling the air with their sweet fragrance. Little trees are growing all
about, surrounding impressive marble statues and fountains, while large trees
with wide crowns spend shade.
name: Garden of the Ainur

desc:
This is a quiet place where those who watch the world below can share their
thoughts on life, death, the landscape and wonders of Arda's mighty warriors and
mysterious wonders of good and evil. Those spending their time here maintain a
respectful tone of voice for others even when disagreeing with the strongest of
adverse opinions. To those who have earned the right to join this collection of
minds, all your thoughts on the future of this land are welcome.
name: The Shapers' Board

desc:
A huge hall stretches away into the distance. Great arched doorways break the
flow of the marbled walls. This is the Nexus: the great hall connecting all of
the areas of Arda together. Above each archway is inscribed the name of the area
to which it gives access.
name: Tolkien Forum

desc:
This is a very small, rectangular room. The walls are empty and made of dim
black stones. In the middle of the room a small model has been placed on an oak
table. The model shows a landscape, coloured and with very very small details,
like trees and even tiny rabbits. In the middle of the model an impenetrable
tower rises above the hills, surrounded by the Misty Mountains. A river runs
wildly south from the tower, and not far south of it a ford can be seen, guarded
by men on horses. Somehow the model still leaves something for your imagination,
it doesn't tell the full tale, something urges you to stay here and look at it
to find out what is amiss.
name: Isengard/Dunland

desc:
For a room which is supposed to represent the cream of great ideas, the contents
of the room seem curiously devoid of any meaningful structure.
name: Idea Board

desc:
This is a most unpleasant room. Millions of ugly little bugs, all different, are
constantly crawling on the floor, pouring endlessly out of holes and cracks in
the walls, and eventually up your legs, except those who hang from the ceiling
and drop down into your hair instead. Fortunately, a huge battery of anti-bug
equipment is hanging on the walls: Very strong insecticides, sticky paper tape,
big aerosol cans of DDT, and several tame venus flytraps. Next to the equipment
is a bug-proof sign: "All bugs are reported to this board. Please remove them as
soon as they have been rectified. All builders are required to correct the bugs
in their zones."
name: Bug Board

desc:
To the south of this paved square stands a massive iron gate, with delicate
gilding and simple yet beautiful carvings. The small paving stones continue to
the north, where the path enters an enclosure, and at the centre of this square
a stairway leads down to the Builders' Library.
name: The Entrance to the Ainur's Garden

desc:
Here it is that the builders and supervisors of test zones communicate. They are
often to be seen writing wordy yet wise posts on the board, and yet magically
only they, and no others save the Valar can see these words of wisdom... Those
wishing to build a testzone should read the sign in this room. Most of the other
information (that can be found here and on board 6) is outdated. On a shelf,
there are two important books (look books) concerned with testzone building.
name: Testzone Central

desc:
This untidy room is cluttered with all manner of maps plans and documents. Long
ago it served as the discussion room for miscellaneous zones, but these days it
has fallen into disarray, being used as a rather untidy archive for information
on superzones long completed. The Management apologises for any inconvenience,
but we'd hate for you to be buried alive under all the archived information. For
your own safety, we have restricted access to the hoardes of really useless
documents stored here.
name: Building Archive

desc:
A small hill rises in the green plains as a tactical vantage point. From here
you can espy all comings and goings in the realm of the horse-lords. The land is
moving, something is in the air, suddenly it reaches you in a gust of wind
carrying a solemn monotone chant. The gods are singing of changes to come. At
first the intensity and power of the song overwhelms you, but as you listen
closer and slowly begin to grasp their will a feeling begins to grow within
...... the hope for the coming of the Rohirrim is suddenly rekindled!
name: Rohan

desc:
You find yourself plunged into darkness. All light has suddenly been
extinguished and you find your innards turn. Then after what seems an age, but
is probably no more than an instant, a bright light appears about you, blinding
in its white intensity. A small pool of illumination is created by the beam from
above. You can see nothing else...everything is featureless. You stand alone,
your body blanched by the brightness, naked under its scrutiny. Then it
starts....a sound, faint at first....then little by little it gets louder. As it
grows you start to make out its origin. The sound of metal scraping on stone, as
if someone is deliberately dragging a blade across the hard floor. The scraping
continues...louder and louder it gets, and all the while your mind becomes more
and more focused on the origin. Who is making that sound? Why are they doing it?
Are they drawing nearer? You long for the answers, you will endure anything just
to have this come to an end...for resolution. Then, without notice the sound
stops. Peace for an instant. Then a face appears at the edge of the light
staring into your eyes....the Inquisitor has arrived!
name: Comes the Inquisitor

desc:
Here the builders will find all the help that they need. Take care that things
may change, for instance some unused flags will be reassigned. Some books are
here, available for consultation. Type: "look books" to read! A sign here,
containing information about the books. A large spellbook is also here, bound to
a shelf.
name: The Builders' Library

desc:
This hall hosts discussions about what quests to unleash onto unsuspecting
mortals. In the corner, a couple of Maiar are working on yet another grocery
list. Right above their heads, a menacing-looking cloud is forming.
name: The Hall of Quests

desc:
This is where builders congregate after all the mundane building work is done,
hoping to catch one of the rare breed of mudllers. You notice a look of
hopelessness in some of the weary faces and bloodshot eyes. Still, new builders
keep streaming in with fresh dreams of complexities they can add to their new
creations. To see the status of jobs taken, look jobs. If you have time to
spare, look at the sign, "FAQ" and "jobs".
name: Builders' Waiting Room for Mudlle Effects

desc:
A vortex of Magic energy is pulsating in the middle of the room, displacing the
very laws of nature and holding you in a firm grip, casting magic symbols in
showers everywhere. The symbols, which take the form of arcane pentagrams,
subtly engraved Tengwar lettering and crudely chiseled runes, reveal at closer
examination a delicately woven pattern of braces, commas, parentheses and the
proud but simple theorems of lambda calculus.
name: Chamber of Magic Mudlle Manipulation

desc:
This plain, perhaps even simple chamber is full of very dusty and rotted
simple-looking pieces of code, and of simple rusty tools to craft them. But
those responsible for coding this simple magic, with these simple tools, wish it
to be made clear that they absolutley deny any truth behind the rumour that they
are to be referred to as simpletons.
name: Dilapidated Chamber of Simple Magic

desc:
In the deep foundations of Lugburz, bound about by the power of the One Ring,
stand many impenetrable caverns. They survived the overthrowing of Sauron, but
went unnoticed when he rebuilt his mighty fortress. And here, for a time, you
must dwell, unable to participate in the wars of Middle-earth. A tattered sign
has been fixed to the wall.
name: In a Dungeon beneath Barad-dur

desc:
Though these pits and caverns were delved exceedingly deep beneath the tower of
Lugburz, still somehow, tales from Middle-earth manage to reach you here, from
time to time.
name: In the Pits of Lugburz

desc:
These mighty vaults and caverns, hidden by deceit, deep beneath Barad-dur, are
never reached by the light of the sun. And yet, by some strange means, tales of
the world without sometimes reach you here. Perhaps they are but echoes of
stories long forgotten, or perhaps they are dreams of what is yet to come.
name: In a Hidden Pit beneath Barad-dur

desc:
Whatever this pool is cannot be told. It might be pure evil that has collected
here, deep down below Barad-dur. The surface is perfectly still but it does not
shine like a mirror. It is of an even deeper black that can be imagined and it
seems to go on forever, down into the deepest, forgotten places below Arda,
where unknown evil and power eagerly await the return of Morgoth.
name: The Ancient Pool
